title: Windows Binaries | Include Version
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Binaries version information on Windows "Podman.exe"**

To make easier corporate deployments and detection over MDM solutions, I believe that binaries should have release version on your binaries, such other apps. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman on Windows system

2. Check installed version of binaries
`(Get-Item -Path (Get-Command podman).Source).VersionInfo`

3. FileVersion or Product Version are empty.

**Describe the results you received:**

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1860114/193738887-950468d4-34bc-4443-ab84-620cdaf25e7b.png)
**Describe the results you expected:**

Filled product version and/or file version such WSL in example.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman.exe version 4.2.1
```

**Output of `podman info`:**


![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1860114/193740081-869d7958-e39c-4902-8b66-490e836f5327.png)


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Windows 11 22H2 with WSL2

comments.body:
Hmm, well the windows binary is cross-compiled on Linux.  Maybe there's a build flag we need to add?

The podman version value is exposed (internally) in the `Makefile` as `$(RELEASE_VERSION)` and `$(RELEASE_NUMBER)`.  So if someone knows the right build argument, it should be easy to add.

There is a tool for go language, which will create resources for the linker to embed into windows binaries https://github.com/josephspurrier/goversioninfo

podman-desktop is as a separate case, but I believe there should an equivalent for Electron apps.

I think then winssh-proxy also should be added to the scope.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
In case it helps, there's also a Makefile target in the podman-remote build chain that kicks out a 'version' binary.  When executed, it will splat out the current podman version to stdout (IIRC).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Still relevant but haven’t gotten to it.
